blocks:
Ayahs

وَٱلطُّورِ (۱)
By the Mount [Sinai], (1)
وَكِتَـٰبٍ مَّسْطُورٍ (۲)
by the Book inscribed (2)
فِى رَقٍّ مَّنشُورٍ (۳)
on an unrolled parchment; (3)
وَٱلْبَيْتِ ٱلْمَعْمُورِ (٤)
by the House greatly frequented; (4)
وَٱلسَّقْفِ ٱلْمَرْفُوعِ (٥)
by the vault raised high, (5)
وَٱلْبَحْرِ ٱلْمَسْجُورِ (٦)
by the surging sea: (6)
إِنَّ عَذَابَ رَبِّكَ لَوَٰقِعٌ (۷)
indeed your Lord’s punishment will surely befall. (7)
مَّا لَهُۥ مِن دَافِعٍ (۸)
There is none who can avert it. (8)

Key Points

Ṭūr (The Mount) literally means mountain, but apparently, what is meant here is a mountain in the Sinai region which was the appointed meeting place (Mīqāt) between Allah and Prophet Moses. Mount Ṭūr is a sacred place and is described in the Quran as: «الطور الایمن» (1), «بالواد المقدّس» (2), and «فى البقعة المباركة» (3).

What is meant by «كتاب مسطور» (the inscribed Book) are the heavenly Books, such as the Torah and the Quran, which were written on thin sheets of parchment and were, of course, open and unfolded, not rolled up and closed. «فى رقّ منشور» (on an unrolled parchment). Raqq (parchment) comes from riqqat (thinness) and refers to a skin prepared for writing, which is fine and thin. Manshūr means spread out or unfolded, and Masjūr means kindled, blazing, or aflame; it has also been interpreted to mean full or overflowing.

The intent of «بیت المعمور» (the Frequented House) is the House of Allah (the Ka'bah), which has always been ma'mūr (frequented and prosperous) due to the presence of pilgrims and Hajj performers. The Ka'bah is mentioned ten times in the Quran as Bayt (House), such as: «البیت العتیق» (4). Of course, narrations state that there is also a place in the heavens directly above the Ka'bah around which the angels circumambulate, and that too is called «بیت المعمور» (5).

The intent of «البحر المسجور» (the Blazing Sea) is the blazing, boiling, and roaring sea, or the seas that will be kindled on the verge of the Day of Judgment, as we read in Ayah 6 of Sūrat At-Takwīr: «و اذا البحار سُجّرت». Or it refers to the molten and boiling materials deep within the earth that, like a sea of fire, occasionally erupt from the mouths of volcanoes.

In the last Ayah of the previous Sūrah (Adh-Dhāriyāt), we read: «یومهم الّذى یوعدون» (their Day which they are promised), which spoke of the promise of the Resurrection. This Sūrah, after five oaths, states that that promise will certainly come to pass. «انّ عذاب ربّك لواقع».

That inanimate stone upon which revelation descends (Mount Ṭūr) is sacred and worthy of an oath. How then can a human being whose heart is the center of revelation lose His sanctity over time? And how can the misguided Wahhabis claim that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, having passed away, is no different from other creatures?!

Swearing by the Book and writing, especially during the Age of Ignorance (Jāhiliyyah), is a sign of the advanced nature of the school of thought, «و كتاب مسطور». In Sūrat Al-Qalam, too, the Quran swears by the pen, the book, and its lines. «ن و القلم و ما یسطرون».

1) Maryam, 52. 2) Taha, 12. 3) Qasas, 30. 4) Hajj, 29. 5) Tafsir Nūr Ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- It is necessary to keep alive the name and memory of, and preserve the sacred places of, the heavenly religions. «و الطور»

2- By swearing by Mount Ṭūr (the place of revelation to Moses), attract the hearts of the People of the Book towards yourselves. «و الطّور»

3- In order to bring conviction to humanity, Allah swears by the systems of legislation (Tashrī') and creation (Takwīn) (the mountain, the Book, the Ka'bah, the sky, and the sea). «والطّور و كتاب مسطور... و السقف المرفوع»

4- Heavenly revelation must be written and presented on the best and finest paper or parchment. «كتاب مسطور فى رقّ منشور»

5- The Holy Book must always be open and utilized. «كتاب... منشور»

6- Beneficial and blessed knowledge is that which is published and spread. «منشور»

7- The Quran pays attention both to the sanctity of the heavenly Books, hence swearing by them, and to the orderliness in their writing, method of presentation, and dissemination. «و كتاب مسطور فى رقّ منشور»

8- The prosperity (ābadānī) of a house is not due to its splendor, but to the traffic, sanctity, and blessing it holds. «البیت المعمور» (The House of Allah is the simplest of houses, yet the most blessed of structures.)

9- By studying nature, we realize Allah's power to bring about the Resurrection. «والسقف المرفوع و البحر المسجور»

10- The Holy Quran directs human attention from the visible to the invisible. «و البحر المسجور»

11- Wrath and punishment are among the attributes of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «عذاب ربّك»

12- The greater the disbelief of the deniers, the stronger the conviction of the believers in the Resurrection must become. «انّ عذاب ربّك لواقع»

13- The descent of heavenly Books «و كتاب مسطور» and the manifestation of divine power in existence «و السّقف المرفوع» must lead to contemplation and faith; otherwise, inattention to them will be a cause for punishment. «انّ عذاب ربّك لواقع»

14- Those afflicted by divine punishment cannot repel the torment from themselves by any means. «ماله من دافع»

Explanations

Portrait of Sūrat Aṭ-Ṭūr

This Sūrah was revealed in Mecca and contains forty-nine Ayahs. The naming of this Sūrah as Aṭ-Ṭūr is due to its first Ayah. The word Ṭūr appears ten times in the Quran, and what is meant by it is Mount Ṭūr, the appointed meeting place (Mīqāt) of Prophet Moses عليه السلام and the location where the Torah was revealed. Like other Meccan Sūrahs, most of the content of this Sūrah concerns the occurrence of the Resurrection, the reward of the righteous, and the punishment of the disbelievers. It also contains references to Monotheism (Tawḥīd), the defense of the Prophet, and patience and steadfastness.

Ayahs

يَوْمَ تَمُورُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ مَوْرًا (۹)
On the day when the sky whirls violently, (9)
وَتَسِيرُ ٱلْجِبَالُ سَيْرًا (۱٠)
and the mountains move with an awful motion: (10)
فَوَيْلٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۱۱)
woe to the deniers on that day (11)
ٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ فِى خَوْضٍ يَلْعَبُونَ (۱۲)
—those who play around in vain talk, (12)
يَوْمَ يُدَعُّونَ إِلَىٰ نَارِ جَهَنَّمَ دَعًّا (۱۳)
the day when they will be shoved forcibly toward the fire of hell, [and told:] (13)
هَـٰذِهِ ٱلنَّارُ ٱلَّتِى كُنتُم بِهَا تُكَذِّبُونَ (۱٤)
‘This is the Fire which you used to deny! (14)

Key Points

The word "Mawr" (مَور) literally means rapid and rotational movement, but one that is disorganized and whirlpool-like, just as the wind scatters and intertwines dust and debris in the air. "Khawdh" (خوض) originally means entering water and passing through it, but technically, it refers to a situation where a person delves into vain and false matters. The word "Da‘‘" (دعّ) means pushing or driving away with violence/harshness. As the Quran states in Surah Al-Ma'un, one of the signs of denying the religion is the rejection of orphans: «أرأیت الّذى یكذب بالدین فذلك الّذى یدعّ الیتیم» (Have you seen him who denies the religion? That is the one who repels the orphan.) The entire universe was created for humanity, and as soon as the record of humanity on Earth is closed, the foundation of existence will also be gathered up. «تمور السّماء مورا» (The heaven will reel and whirl.) Elsewhere, we read: We will roll up and gather the expanse of the heaven like the rolling up of a scroll. «نطوى السّماء كطىّ السّجلّ»(6) Only Allah is permanent and steadfast. The seven firm heavens «و بنینا فوقكم سبعاً شداداً»(7) will also become weak and turn into moving waves, «تمور السّماء مورا», and the mountains that were the cause of the Earth's stability: «و جعلنا فیها رواسى شامخات»(8) will become restless, weak, and flowing on that Day. «و تسیر الجبال» (And the mountains will move.)

6) Al-Anbiya, 104. 7) An-Naba, 12. 8) Al-Mursalat, 27.

Messages

1- Depicting the events of the Day of Judgment is a context for abandoning denial and obstinacy. «تمور السّماء... تسیر الجبال...»

2- For the occurrence of the Day of Judgment, not only the Earth but also the heaven and celestial bodies will be disrupted. «تمور السّماء... تسیر الجبال...»

3- The deniers lack logic; their preoccupation with vanities and absurdities has caused their denial. «للمكذّبین الّذین هم فى خوض یلعبون»

4- Vain talk is abundant, but the danger lies in a person becoming immersed in futility and occupying himself with absurdities. «فى خوض یلعبون»

5- The efforts of the disbelievers to discredit the Quran are a trivial and fruitless endeavor. «للمكذّبین الّذین هم فى خوض یلعبون»

6- The opponents of the prophets will both be dragged to Hell and subjected to reproach and rebuke. «یدعّون... هذه» (They will suffer spiritual, psychological, and physical torment.)

7- By denying the Day of Judgment, the truth will not remain hidden. «هذه النّار...»

8- Stubborn denial results in severe wrath. «مكذّبین... یدعّون الى النّار»

9- The punishment for denial that is continuous and stems from obstinacy is nothing but the Fire. «هذه النّار التى كنتم بها تكذبون»

Ayahs

أَفَسِحْرٌ هَـٰذَآ أَمْ أَنتُمْ لَا تُبْصِرُونَ (۱٥)
Is this, then, [also] magic, or is it you who do not perceive? (15)
ٱصْلَوْهَا فَٱصْبِرُوٓا۟ أَوْ لَا تَصْبِرُوا۟ سَوَآءٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ۖ إِنَّمَا تُجْزَوْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٦)
Enter it, and it will be the same for you whether you are patient or impatient. You are only being requited for what you used to do.’ (16)

Key Points

The polytheists always accused the Prophet of practicing sorcery and magic, claiming that he stole their minds and cast a veil over their eyes to present things to them as miracles and revelation, while all these claims were baseless. Therefore, on the Day of Resurrection, they will be told, by way of reproach and rebuke: Is this also sorcery and magic? Do you not see the Fire and feel its heat? Sometimes the deniers are rebuked using the very expressions they used in this world; they used to say in this world that the work of the prophets was magic, and there they will be questioned about that very matter. «أفسحر هذا» Those who used to tell the prophets: It makes no difference whether you advise us or not. «سواء علینا أ وعظت أم لم تكن من الواعظین»(9) On the Day of Resurrection, they will be told: Whether you endure patiently or not, it makes no difference. «فاصبروا اولا تصبروا سواء علیكم»

9) Surah Ash-Shu'ara, 136.

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection, that which was denied will be displayed before human eyes. «هذه النّار... أفسحر هذا»

2- The root of denial is the lack of correct vision and insight. «ویل یومئذ للمکذبین... ام أنتم لاتبصرون»

3- Continuous denial, «كنتم بها تكذبون», entails continuous humiliation. «یدعّون... أفسحر هذا... لاتبصرون . أصلوها فاصبروا أو لاتصبروا...»

4- The punishment for engaging in vain and frivolous matters is entry into Hell. «فى خوض... أصلوها»

5- The punishments of the Day of Resurrection are just and are nothing but the result of man's own deeds. «انّما تجزون ما كنتم تعملون»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ فِى جَنَّـٰتٍ وَنَعِيمٍ (۱۷)
Indeed the Godwary will be amid gardens and bliss, (17)
فَـٰكِهِينَ بِمَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ وَوَقَىٰهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْجَحِيمِ (۱۸)
rejoicing because of what their Lord has given them, and that their Lord has saved them from the punishment of hell. (18)
كُلُوا۟ وَٱشْرَبُوا۟ هَنِيٓـًٔۢا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۱۹)
[They will be told:] ‘Enjoy your food and drink, [as a reward] for what you used to do.’ (19)
مُتَّكِـِٔينَ عَلَىٰ سُرُرٍ مَّصْفُوفَةٍ ۖ وَزَوَّجْنَـٰهُم بِحُورٍ عِينٍ (۲٠)
They will be reclining on arrayed couches, and We will wed them to big-eyed houris. (20)

Key Points

«هَنیئاً» means something that involves no hardship or toil and becomes pleasant and agreeable to the human being. This is contrary to worldly blessings, which are usually accompanied by hardship and difficulty either before or after them. «حور عین» (Houris) is composed of two words: «حور», the plural of «حوراء», meaning black-eyed, and the word «عین», the plural of «عیناء», meaning beautiful-eyed. The spouses of Paradise possess large, black, and beautiful eyes. Companionship with the pure and the righteous, and conversing with them, is one of the pleasures of Paradise, which the Quran has referred to in various instances regarding these gatherings of intimacy and joy held upon the couches of Paradise. Verses 16 and 19, which relate to the punishment and reward of the dwellers of Hell and Paradise, are very similar to each other, with one very subtle difference: regarding the dwellers of Hell, we read: «انما تجزون ما كنتم تعملون» (You are only being recompensed for what you used to do), but regarding the dwellers of Paradise, we read: «هنیئاً بما كنتم تعملون» (Eat and drink with pleasure because of what you used to do). The first uses «ما كنتم» and the second uses «بما كنتم». Yes, the punishment of the dwellers of Hell is commensurate with their deeds, and no more, especially since it is mentioned alongside the word «انّما» (only), meaning your punishment is the very essence of your deed. But the reward of the dwellers of Paradise is far greater than their performance, and their deed is merely a means and a pretext for receiving all that grace. «هنیئاً بما كنتم تعملون»

Messages

1- The key to Paradise is piety (Taqwa). «انّ المتقین فى جنّات»

2- Comparing the final outcome of the good and the wicked is one of the best methods for recognizing truth and falsehood. «هذه النّار... فى جنّات و نعیم»

3- Various kinds of gardens and blessings are prepared for the people of Paradise. «جنات و نعیم»

4- Sometimes blessings exist in this world, but for various reasons, a person cannot fully enjoy them; however, in Paradise, both the blessing and the enjoyment exist. «جنات... فاكهین»

5- Blessings bring greater pleasure when they are bestowed by Allah as an act of love and grace «آتاهم ربّهم», and when they are free from bitterness and anxiety. «و وقاهم ربّهم»

6- Whoever protected himself through piety in this world, Allah will protect him from the torment of Hellfire on the Day of Judgment. «المتقین... وقاهم»

7- The pious should not be proud of their piety and deeds, for all blessings and salvation come from Allah. «أتاهم ربّهم... وقاهم ربّهم»

8- Freedom and honor increase the pleasure of the blessing. «كلوا و اشربوا هنیئاً» The dwellers of Paradise have no restrictions in eating and drinking and are honored with the phrase «هنیئاً».

9- Enjoyment, under the shadow of security, increases pleasure. «وقاهم ربّهم... كلوا واشربوا»

10- Worldly foods are accompanied by difficulties in their preparation, preservation, and consumption; in the blessings of the Hereafter, there is never any difficulty. «هنیئاً»

11- Cheerfulness and contentment alongside the use of blessings are conditions for complete enjoyment. «فى جنّات و نعیم فاكهین... متّكئین على سُرر...»

12- Those who averted their gaze from non-mahram (unrelated) individuals in this world will have beautiful-faced spouses in the Hereafter. «المتقّین... زوّجناهم بحورعین»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَٱتَّبَعَتْهُمْ ذُرِّيَّتُهُم بِإِيمَـٰنٍ أَلْحَقْنَا بِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَمَآ أَلَتْنَـٰهُم مِّنْ عَمَلِهِم مِّن شَىْءٍ ۚ كُلُّ ٱمْرِئٍۭ بِمَا كَسَبَ رَهِينٌ (۲۱)
The faithful and their descendants who followed them in faith—We will make their descendants join them, and We will not stint anything from [the reward of] their deeds. Every person is hostage to what he has earned. (21)
وَأَمْدَدْنَـٰهُم بِفَـٰكِهَةٍ وَلَحْمٍ مِّمَّا يَشْتَهُونَ (۲۲)
We will provide them with fruits and meat, such as they desire. (22)

Key Points

«Alatnā» (ألَتنا) is derived from the root lūt (لوت), meaning deficiency or reduction. «Mā alatnāhum min ‘amalihim» (ما ألتناهم من عملهم) means that due to joining the children to their fathers, We will not diminish anything from the reward of the fathers.

The word «rahīn» (رهین) has come with two meanings: pledge (or collateral) and companion. When it says that man is rahīn (pledged/bound) to his deeds, it means he is inseparable from and accompanied by his deeds.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام regarding the Ayah «Wa alladhīna āmanū wa atba‘athum...» (و الّذین آمنوا و اتّبعتهم...) said: The reference is to children whose deeds are less than their fathers', but Allah joins them to their fathers so that they may be a source of joy and comfort for them. (10)

Allah first joins the believing progeny of the faithful individuals to them, «Alladhīna āmanū... alḥaqnā bihim dhurrīyatahum» (الّذین آمنوا... الحقنا بهم ذرّیتهم), and then increases the blessings and provisions there. «Amadadnāhum bifākihatin» (أمددناهم بفاكهة).

Characteristics of the fruits of Paradise: 1. Abundance. «Fākihatun kathīratun» (فاكهة كثیرة) (11) 2. Harmlessness/Safety. «Fākihatun āminīn» (فاكهة آمنین) (12) 3. Paired nature. «Min kulli fākihah zawjān» (من كلّ فاكهة زوجان) (13) 4. Permanence. «Amadadnāhum bifākihatin» (أمددناهم بفاكهة) (14) 5. Permissibility/Unrestricted access. «Wa lā mamnū‘ah» (و لاممنوعة) (15) 6. Generous hospitality. «Fawākih wa hum mukramūn» (فواكِهَ و هم مكرمون) (16) 7. Variety. «Fawākih mimmā yashtahūn» (فواكِهَ مما یشتهون) (17) 8. Accompanied by non-fruit items (meat). «Bifākihatin wa laḥm» (بفاكهة و لحم) (18) 9. Selectivity/Choice. «Wa fākihatin mimmā yatakhayyarūn» (و فاكهة مما یتخیّرون) (19) 10. Easy access. «Quṭūfuhā dāniyah» (قطوفها دانیة) (20), «Wa dhullilat quṭūfuhā tadhīlan» (و ذلّلت قطوفها تذلیلا) (21)

Furthermore, the fruits of Paradise have no residue, no need for excretion, no change in taste, and no difficulty in picking.

10) كافى، ج 3، ص 250. 11) زخرف، 73. 12) دخان، 55. 13) الرّحمن، 52 . 14) طور، 22. 15) واقعه، 33. 16) صافّات، 42. 17) مرسلات، 42. 18) طور، 42. 19) واقعه، 20. 20) حاقّه، 23. 21) انسان، 14.

Messages

1- In Islam, the axis is faith, not family or relatives. «Wa atba‘athum dhurrīyatuhum bi-īmān» (واتبعتهم ذریتهم بایمان)

2- The result of joining the righteous path of one's ancestors in this world is being joined with them in Paradise. «Wa atba‘athum... alḥaqnā bihim» (و اتبعتهم... الحقنا بهم)

3- Being accompanied by one's descendants and children is one of the pleasures of Paradise. «Alḥaqnā bihim dhurrīyatahum» (الحقنا بهم ذریتهم)

4- The affection for children exists even in the Hereafter. «Alḥaqnā bihim dhurrīyatahum» (الحقنا بهم ذریتهم)

5- Joining children (to the family) in this world may cause housing to become cramped or lead to a loss of comfort, but joining them in Paradise entails no deficiency whatsoever. «Wa mā alatnāhum... min shay’» (و ما التناهم... من شى ء)

6- The joining of a person's progeny to him is among the effects of good deeds. «Alḥaqnā... bimā kasaba rahīn» (الحقنا... بما كسب رهین)

7- The consumption of fruit precedes meat (in mention). «Bifākihatin wa laḥm» (بفاكهة و لحم)

8- In Paradise, too, there are different tastes and desires, and blessings are granted to everyone according to what he wishes. «Mimmā yashtahūn» (مما یشتهون)

Ayahs

يَتَنَـٰزَعُونَ فِيهَا كَأْسًا لَّا لَغْوٌ فِيهَا وَلَا تَأْثِيمٌ (۲۳)
There they will pass from hand to hand a cup wherein there will be neither any vain talk nor sinful speech. (23)
۞ وَيَطُوفُ عَلَيْهِمْ غِلْمَانٌ لَّهُمْ كَأَنَّهُمْ لُؤْلُؤٌ مَّكْنُونٌ (۲٤)
They will be waited upon by their youths, as if they were guarded pearls. (24)
وَأَقْبَلَ بَعْضُهُمْ عَلَىٰ بَعْضٍ يَتَسَآءَلُونَ (۲٥)
They will turn to one another, questioning each other. (25)
قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّا كُنَّا قَبْلُ فِىٓ أَهْلِنَا مُشْفِقِينَ (۲٦)
They will say, ‘Indeed, aforetime, we used to be apprehensive about our families. (26)
فَمَنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْنَا وَوَقَىٰنَا عَذَابَ ٱلسَّمُومِ (۲۷)
But Allah showed us favour and He saved us from the punishment of the [infernal] miasma. (27)
إِنَّا كُنَّا مِن قَبْلُ نَدْعُوهُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥ هُوَ ٱلْبَرُّ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (۲۸)
We used to supplicate Him aforetime. Indeed He is the All-benign, the All-merciful.’ (28)

Key Points

"Kās" (كأس) is a full cup, and "Qadah" (قَدح) is an empty cup. The word "mushfiq" (مشفق), if used with the particle min (مِن), means fear and anxiety, and if used with the particle fī (فى), it means attention and care. "Yatanāza'ūn" (یتنازعون) means taking the cup from one another's hands with jest and affection, similar to what happens among friends during wedding nights. Worldly wine removes a person's intellect and leads him to babbling and vain talk, besides stimulating sexual urges and driving a person toward sin. Therefore, the Quran states: the people of Paradise offer each other cups other than [worldly] wine, which neither causes vain talk nor sin, but rather, in the words of the Quran, is "شراباً طهوراً" (a purifying drink) (22).

The phrase "lu'lu'in maknūn" (لؤلؤ مكنون - hidden pearls) appears twice in the Quran: once regarding the spouses of Paradise (23) and once regarding the servants of Paradise (24).

"Samūm" (سموم) refers to a hot, scorching wind that affects the parts of the body like poison (sam). We read in a hadith: If an opening the size of a fingertip from that wind were opened upon the people of the earth, it would burn the earth and everyone upon it (25).

The Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله said to Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام: The beautiful youths who are the servants of the people of Paradise are the children of the disbelievers (kuffār) and polytheists (mushrikūn) who are neither criminals to be taken to Hell nor righteous to enjoy the blessings of Paradise; these individuals will appear as servants in Paradise (26).

22) Insan, 21. 23) Waqi'ah, 23. 24) Tur, 24. 25) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur. 26) Bihar, Vol 5, p 291.

Messages

1- Recreation is permissible provided it is free from vanity (laghw) and sin. «یتنازعون... لا لغو... و لاتأثیم»

2- Although rivers of milk, honey, wine, and water flow in Paradise, the people of Paradise are served with special vessels, because suitable vessels increase the pleasure of drinking. «كأساً»

3- No vain matter, amusement (lahw), or sin has a place in Paradise. «لا لغو فیها ولا تأثیم»

4- Any action that is free from vanity and sin is permissible. «لا لغو فیها ولا تأثیم»

5- The servants of Paradise serve lovingly and like moths (around a flame). «یطوف...»

6- The servants of Paradise are not borrowed or temporary; they are special servants. «غلمان لهم»

7- The beauty of the attendants in Paradise is itself another blessing. «كانهم لؤلؤ مكنون»

8- Covering beautiful and valuable things is a virtue. «لؤلؤ مكنون»

9- Among the pleasures of Paradise is the conversation of its inhabitants with one another. «واقبل بعضهم على بعض یتسائلون»

10- The key to Paradise must be sought in this world. «كنّا قبل فى أهلنا»

11- Compassion and tenderness, especially towards family, is the key to Paradise. «فى أهلنا مشفقین»

12- A person must have attention, commitment, concern, and fervor regarding his family. «فى أهلنا مشفقین»

13- The people of Paradise consider Paradise to be a divine favor (minnah), not the reward for their own deeds. «فمنّ اللّه علینا»

14- One must turn to and call upon Him who is the source of goodness and mercy. «ندعوه انه هو البّر الرحیم»

15- There is no source for goodness and mercy except Allah. «انه هو البرّ الرحیم» (The word "Huwa" indicates the exclusivity of goodness and mercy to Him.)

16- Allah's goodness stems from His mercy (not to gain benefit, ease conscience, or meet the expectations of others). «البرّ الرحیم»

17- The entry of the righteous (muttaqīn) into Paradise and their salvation from the torment of Hell is a manifestation of Allah's goodness and mercy. «فمنّ اللّه علینا و وقانا عذاب السموم... انه هو البرّ الرحیم»

Ayahs

فَذَكِّرْ فَمَآ أَنتَ بِنِعْمَتِ رَبِّكَ بِكَاهِنٍ وَلَا مَجْنُونٍ (۲۹)
So admonish. By your Lord’s blessing, you are not a soothsayer, nor mad. (29)
أَمْ يَقُولُونَ شَاعِرٌ نَّتَرَبَّصُ بِهِۦ رَيْبَ ٱلْمَنُونِ (۳٠)
Do they say, ‘[He is] a poet, for whom we await a fatal accident’? (30)
قُلْ تَرَبَّصُوا۟ فَإِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُتَرَبِّصِينَ (۳۱)
Say, ‘Wait! I too am waiting along with you.’ (31)

Key Points

A "Kāhin" (soothsayer/diviner) is someone who claims knowledge of the future, asserts awareness of secrets and the unseen (ghayb), and claims connection with the jinn. "Rayb al-Manūn" (vicissitudes of fate) is a term meaning the bitter events of time that lead to someone's death. The polytheists, when calling the prophets "Majnūn" (madman), did not mean senseless or foolish, especially since in these verses, "Majnūn" is mentioned alongside "Kāhin" (diviner) and "Shā'ir" (poet), who are intelligent and perceptive individuals. Rather, their intention behind "Majnūn" was someone possessed by jinn, because they believed that diviners and poets, through connection with the jinn, possessed the power of prophecy and poetry composition—a feat ordinary people could not achieve. Evidence for this meaning is that in one verse, the descriptions of poet and madman appear together: «لشاعر مجنون»(27)

27) Saffat, 36.

Messages

1- One should not fear the slanders and false accusations of the disbelievers (such as: Kāhin, Shā'ir, and Majnūn), but must continue the duty of warning and reminding. «فذكّر فما انت... بكاهن...»

2- Allah protects His chosen ones and defends them. «فما انت بنعمة ربّك بكاهن و لامجنون»

3- The preacher of religion is the target of every kind of slander. «بكاهن و لامجنون... یقولون شاعر»

4- Any fault or defect that is kept away from a person is due to divine grace and favor. «فما أنت بنعمة ربّك بكاهن و لامجنون...»

5- Diviners (Kāhin) and fortune-tellers are far from Allah's mercy. «فما أنت بنعمة ربّك بكاهن»

6- The enemy is not satisfied with a single accusation and is constantly seeking to strike. «بكاهن، مجنون، شاعر»

7- The enemy awaits the death of the great figures of religion; therefore, action must be taken to frustrate their hopes for the period following the death of religious personalities. «نتربّص به ریب المنون»

8- The religious leader must not abandon the scene due to the enemies' slanders; rather, he must remain and defend. «نتربّص به ریب المنون»

9- Believers, just like the enemies, must have hope and a plan to achieve victory. «نتربّص به... فانّى معكم من المتربّصین»

Ayahs

أَمْ تَأْمُرُهُمْ أَحْلَـٰمُهُم بِهَـٰذَآ ۚ أَمْ هُمْ قَوْمٌ طَاغُونَ (۳۲)
Is it their intellect which prompts them to [say] this, or are they a rebellious lot? (32)
أَمْ يَقُولُونَ تَقَوَّلَهُۥ ۚ بَل لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۳۳)
Do they say, ‘He has improvised it [himself]?’ Rather, they have no faith! (33)
فَلْيَأْتُوا۟ بِحَدِيثٍ مِّثْلِهِۦٓ إِن كَانُوا۟ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۳٤)
Let them bring a discourse like it, if they are truthful. (34)

Key Points

In these verses and the verses following them, Allah poses fourteen reproachful questions to the opponents of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, closing off all paths that might somehow serve as an excuse for the disbelievers not to submit. Perhaps nowhere else in the Quran are opponents criticized and their excuses addressed in this manner. Aḥlām (أحلام) is the plural of ḥilm (حِلم), meaning intellect (or reason). Since the elders of Quraysh considered themselves people of intellect and thought, the Quran states in this verse: Does their intellect command them to consider the Quran magic and sorcery? No, rather they are rebellious against the truth. Taqawwul (تقوُّل) means fabricating a statement oneself and attributing it to someone else. Ḥadīth (حدیث) means "new." Since the contents of the Quran are novel, it is referred to as Ḥadīth. Allah has repeatedly challenged the disbelievers: sometimes requesting them to bring a book like the Quran, "فأتوا بكتاب من عنداللّه" (28); sometimes reducing the challenge and settling for bringing ten chapters, "فأتوا بعشر سور مثله" (29); and sometimes [settling for] bringing one chapter, "فأتوا بسورة من مثله" (30); and in verse 34, He even settled for bringing forth discourse similar to it: "فلیأتوا بحدیث مثله"

28) Qasas, 49. 29) Hud, 13. 30) Baqarah, 23.

Messages

1- The accusations of sorcerer, soothsayer, poet, and madman leveled against the Prophet were not based on reason and reflection upon the verses of the Quran. "ام تامرهم احلامهم"

2- The root of disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) is rebellion and transgression against the truth, not intellect or thought. "ام هم قوم طاغون"

3- Anyone whose speech is illogical and who insists upon it is a transgressor. "ام تامرهم احلامهم بهذا ام هم قوم طاغون"

4- The polytheists justified their disbelief through slander and fabrication. "یقولون تقوّله بل لایؤمنون"

5- Man must act either based on intellect or based on revelation, and the disbelievers have deprived themselves of both. "ام تامرهم احلامهم... بل لا یؤمنون"

6- In the storm of accusations, do not abandon logic and argumentation, and challenge the opponents to produce discourse similar to the Quran. "فلیأتوا بحدیث مثله..."

7- The Quran possesses the strongest argument; if the Prophet fabricated the Quran alone and attributed it to Allah, "تقوّله," then all of you collectively should bring forth one discourse like it. "فلیأتوا بحدیث مثله"

8- Challenging opponents is also necessary in the cultural arena. "فلیأتوا بحدیث مثله"

Ayahs

أَمْ خُلِقُوا۟ مِنْ غَيْرِ شَىْءٍ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْخَـٰلِقُونَ (۳٥)
Were they created from nothing? Or are they [their own] creators? (35)
أَمْ خَلَقُوا۟ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ ۚ بَل لَّا يُوقِنُونَ (۳٦)
Did they create the heavens and the earth? Rather, they have no certainty! (36)

Key Points

Man should not abandon his guidance (Irshad) and argumentation (Istidlal) in the face of various accusations. Although various accusations were leveled against the noble Prophet of Islam in the previous verses, Allah, in these verses, awakens their dormant conscience by posing several questions. The Quran presents the most important philosophical discussions in the simplest form of expression. In this noble Ayah, two hypotheses are presented: first, that man was created without a cause, «خُلِقوا من غیر شى ء» (Were they created by nothing?); second, that man created himself. Both hypotheses are rationally impossible. This is because man is a phenomenon (a contingent being), and every phenomenon requires a cause. Furthermore, how can a human being who did not exist become the cause of his own existence?! The polytheists knew that the Creator of the heavens and the earth is Allah. The Quran has stated this matter repeatedly: if the polytheists are asked who created the heavens and the earth, they will say: Allah. Therefore, the main question posed by these verses is: why do you not believe in what has been sent by your Creator for your guidance, and why do you disobey?

Messages

1- The method of posing questions to awaken the conscience, mind, and thought of human beings is one of the Quranic methods in discussions of theology (knowledge of Allah). «أم خُلقوا... أم هم الخالقون»

2- If man did not exist, how did He become the cause of His own existence? And if He did exist, how did He create Himself again? «أم خلقوا من غیر شى ء أم هم الخالقون»

3- Ask questions in such a way that everyone feels your logical answer within their own conscience. «أم خلقوا السّموات والارض»

4- Man is neither His own creator nor the creator of anything other than Himself. «أم هم الخالقون أم خلقوا السّموات...»

5- Obstinacy leads man to a point where, even if He reaches an argumentative dead end, He does not surrender and does not attain belief and certainty. «بل لایوقنون»

Ayahs

أَمْ عِندَهُمْ خَزَآئِنُ رَبِّكَ أَمْ هُمُ ٱلْمُصَۣيْطِرُونَ (۳۷)
Do they possess the treasuries of your Lord? Or do they control [them]? (37)
أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ ۖ فَلْيَأْتِ مُسْتَمِعُهُم بِسُلْطَـٰنٍ مُّبِينٍ (۳۸)
Do they have a ladder [leading up to heaven] whereby they eavesdrop? If so, let their eavesdropper produce a manifest authority. (38)
أَمْ لَهُ ٱلْبَنَـٰتُ وَلَكُمُ ٱلْبَنُونَ (۳۹)
Does He have daughters while you have sons? (39)

Key Points

The polytheists accepted Allah's creatorship but did not consider Him the Lord of themselves or the universe, and they refused to accept the Prophet. These verses, by posing questions, rebuke them as to why they do not accept the Prophet's words. Is Allah's wealth and power in your possession, or have you yourselves found a way to the heavens and receive revelation directly?! In the Age of Ignorance, people considered the angels to be the daughters of God. Allah rebukes them for how they chose daughters for God and sons for themselves in this division, as stated in other verses: «تلك اذا قسمة ضیزى»

Messages

1- The enemy must be logically disarmed. Therefore, the Quran clarifies the reason for their rebellion and lack of faith by posing several futile questions: «ام عندهم... ام هم المصیطرون ام لهم سلّم...»

2- Man must accept logical speech from anyone. If the opponents have also received some information, we are ready to listen to their words. «فلیأت مستمعهم بسلطان مبین»

3- O Prophet! Do not fear accusations, for they even accuse Allah. «ام له البنات و لكم البنون»

Ayahs

أَمْ تَسْـَٔلُهُمْ أَجْرًا فَهُم مِّن مَّغْرَمٍ مُّثْقَلُونَ (٤٠)
Do you ask them for a reward, so that they are [wary of] being weighed down with debt? (40)
أَمْ عِندَهُمُ ٱلْغَيْبُ فَهُمْ يَكْتُبُونَ (٤۱)
Do they have [access to] the Unseen, which they write down? (41)
أَمْ يُرِيدُونَ كَيْدًا ۖ فَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ هُمُ ٱلْمَكِيدُونَ (٤۲)
Do they seek to outmaneuver [Allah]? But it is the faithless who are the outmaneuvered ones! (42)
أَمْ لَهُمْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَ ٱللَّهِ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (٤۳)
Do they have any god other than Allah? Clear is Allah of any partners that they may ascribe [to Him]! (43)

Key Points

Maghram refers to financial losses that afflict a person without justification, and Muthqal means bearing hardship and a heavy burden. There is no partner in the domain of Divine power, because: - The treasures belong to Him. «وللّه خزائن السّموات و الارض»(31) - Creation belongs to Him. «اللّه خالق كلّ شى ء»(32) - Knowledge of the unseen belongs to Him. «وللّه غیب السّموات و الارض»(33) - Sovereignty and dominion belong to Him. «للّه ملك السّموات و الارض...»(34) Regarding some questions, do not wait for an answer; sometimes the question itself carries the message. In these verses, Allah has posed various questions, none of which are answered, because the goal of the question was not to receive a reply, but rather to awaken the opponents. It must be noted that financial obligations in Islam, such as Khums (one-fifth) and Zakat (alms), are not the wage of prophethood, but rather are for administering society, establishing social justice, and resolving various problems.

31) Al-Munafiqun, 7. 32) Az-Zumar, 62. 33) Hud, 123. 34) Al-Ma'idah, 17.

Messages

1- The Prophets did not request wages or rewards from the people. «ام تسئلهم اجراً»

2- People are sensitive to the demand for payment by the religious preacher; this characteristic must be considered in propagation programs. «فهم من مَغرم مثقلون»

3- In guiding and inviting people to the truth, one must avoid raising material issues that neutralize the effects of propagation or constitute a type of pressure on the people. «فهم من مغرم مثقلون»

4- Utilizing voluntary aid that stems from love, knowledge, and enthusiasm, and which people pay themselves, is permissible. What is negative is demanding wages from the people. «تسئلهم اجراً... مثقلون»

5- The opponents of the Prophets have neither scientific and rational support nor support from the realm of the unseen. «أم عندهم الغیب»

6- One of the methods of invitation and guidance is the question-and-answer method, and the Quran has clarified how to ask questions. «أم... أم... أم... أم...»

7- The plotting and deception of enemies is not temporary, but permanent. «یریدون كیداً»

8- The result of the plotting returns to the individuals themselves. «یریدون كیداً... هم المكیدون»

9- Let us summarize our questions and goals. «ام لهم اله غیر اللّه»

Ayahs

وَإِن يَرَوْا۟ كِسْفًا مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ سَاقِطًا يَقُولُوا۟ سَحَابٌ مَّرْكُومٌ (٤٤)
Were they to see a fragment falling from the sky, they would say, ‘A cumulous cloud.’ (44)
فَذَرْهُمْ حَتَّىٰ يُلَـٰقُوا۟ يَوْمَهُمُ ٱلَّذِى فِيهِ يُصْعَقُونَ (٤٥)
So leave them until they encounter the day when they will be thunderstruck, (45)
يَوْمَ لَا يُغْنِى عَنْهُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا هُمْ يُنصَرُونَ (٤٦)
the day when their guile will not avail them in any way, nor will they be helped. (46)

Key Points

"كِسف" (kisaf) means a piece, and what is meant by it here is the descent of a piece of stone from the sky as divine punishment. "مَركوم" (markūm) means accumulated or piled up, and "یُصعقون" (yuṣ‘aqūn) means destruction and unconsciousness that occurs due to the thunderbolt of punishment. This word is presented in the passive voice, which indicates compulsion and helplessness. Obstinate people constantly make excuses. They requested the descent of a piece (of the sky) from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, [saying]: "فاسقط علینا كسفا..." (35) But if their request were fulfilled, they would still say: This is an accumulated cloud. "و ان یروا... یقولوا سحاب مركوم" Enjoining good and forbidding evil has stages and limitations. Sometimes admonition is necessary, and sometimes turning away and abandonment (of the effort) is required. In Ayah 29, we read: "فذكّر" (So remind), meaning, remind the people! But here we read: "فذرهم" (So leave them), meaning, abandon them to their own state, because guidance no longer has an effect.

35) Ash-Shu'ara, 187.

Messages

1- Obstinacy leads man to incorrect analysis. "كسفا من السّماء... یقولوا سحاب مركوم"

2- A person who denies obvious truths and does not accept even what he sees with his eyes is no longer worthy of guidance. "یقولوا سحاب مركوم فذرهم"

3- Not all individuals are worthy of guidance. "فذرهم"

4- One should not quickly tire of guiding people. The Quran issues the command to abandon them only after posing fourteen questions and closing all avenues of excuse and pretext. "أم تأمرهم... أم... أم... أم... فذرهم"

5- The grace of Allah is such that that even when abandoning man to his own state, He gives the necessary warning. "فذرهم حتّى یلاقوا یومهم الّذى..."

6- Disbelief and obstinacy lead to a bad outcome. "حتّى یلاقوا یومهم..."

7- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah regarding opponents is not haste or rush. "فذرهم حتّى یلاقوایومهم..."

8- When divine wrath arrives, the criminals no longer have control over themselves and must accept it with humiliation. "یصعقون"

9- At the time of divine wrath, neither internal cunning nor external helper is effective. "یوم لایغنى..."

Ayah

وَإِنَّ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ عَذَابًا دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٤۷)
Indeed there is a punishment besides that for those who do wrong, but most of them do not know. (47)

Key Points

Considering the preceding verses and this verse, rejecting the invitation of the prophets is oppression (zulm). Perhaps the intent is that the disbelievers (kuffar) are of two groups: a group that is merely disbelieving, and a group that, in addition to disbelief, commits oppression (zulm) and conspiracy (plot), and the punishment (adhāb) for this group is greater. In Tafsir Qummi, it is stated that the meaning of "عذاباً دون ذلك" (a punishment short of that) is the punishment during the time of Raj'ah (Return), concerning those who committed oppression against the Family of Muhammad علیهم السلام.

Messages

1- For the oppressors, in addition to the Hereafter, there is also punishment (adhāb) in this world. «عذاباً دون ذلك»

2- The oppressors are unaware of their ominous fate in this world. «اكثرهم لایعلمون»

Ayahs

وَٱصْبِرْ لِحُكْمِ رَبِّكَ فَإِنَّكَ بِأَعْيُنِنَا ۖ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ (٤۸)
So submit patiently to the judgement of your Lord, for indeed you fare before Our eyes. And celebrate the praise of your Lord when you rise [at dawn], (48)
وَمِنَ ٱلَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ وَإِدْبَـٰرَ ٱلنُّجُومِ (٤۹)
and also glorify Him during the night and at the receding of the stars. (49)

Key Points

This Surah began with an oath by the place of supplication, "والطور" (By the Mount), and ended with the command for supplication. "و من الّیل فسبّحه و أدبار النّجوم" (And during the night, glorify Him, and at the fading of the stars). Although all the deeds and thoughts of humans are under Allah's supervision, Allah pays special attention to His chosen servants (Awliya'). For example, He tells Noah عليه السلام: The building of your ark is under Our supervision. "اِصنع الفلك باعیننا" (Construct the ark under Our eyes). And He tells the Prophet of Islam: Your entire being is under Our supervision. "فانّك بأعیُننا" (For verily, you are in Our eyes). Glorification (Tasbih) is more important than praise (Hamd); the command for Tasbih was mentioned twice, but the praise of Allah only once. The meaning of "ادبار النجوم" (fading of the stars) is the time of dawn (Fajr), when the stars disappear in the light of the morning. Commentators have interpreted the phrase "من الّیل فسبّحه" as the night prayer (Salat al-Layl) during the pre-dawn hours (Sahar). And in a Hadith, we read: The meaning of "ادبار النجوم" is the two Rak'ahs of supererogatory prayer (Nafilah) before the morning prayer (Salat al-Subh).(36)

36) Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 444.

Messages

1- One must show patience and resistance against obstinate individuals. Yes, propagating the religion requires patience and forbearance in the face of denials and persecutions. "واصبر لحكم ربّك"

2- Patience is valuable if it is in the path of executing the Divine command; otherwise, it is considered stubbornness, obstinacy, and an anti-value. "واصبر لحكم ربّك"

3- The decree and command of Allah are aimed at the training and growth of man. "لحكم ربّك"

4- Even the Prophet is in need of Allah's support. "فانّك باعیننا"

5- What makes a person patient is knowing that he is under Allah's supervision. "فانّك باعیننا"

6- The remembrance of Allah, His praise, and His glorification are the source of patience and steadfastness. "واصبر... و سبّح"

7- The glorification (Tasbih) of Allah precedes His praise (Hamd); first, we must declare Him free from imperfection, and then praise Him.

8- Nightly supplication prepares man for daily effort. "واصبر... و من الّیل فسبّحه..."

9- Specific times for prayer and supplication have greater effects. "حین تقوم و من الّیل... ادبار النجوم"
